sections.explanation: One of the world's earliest urban civilizations (c. 2500–1750 BCE). Known for grid-based town planning and advanced drainage.
facts: Harappa was the first site discovered.
Mohenjo-daro features the 'Great Bath'.
Evidence of maritime trade with Mesopotamia.

sections.explanation: Period when the Vedas were composed (c. 1500–500 BCE). Transitioned from early pastoralism to complex social hierarchies.
facts: Rig Veda is the oldest religious text.
Sama Veda is the Veda of melodies/chants.
Yajur Veda contains sacrificial formulas.
Atharva Veda deals with everyday life/charms.

sections.explanation: Five dynasties that ruled from Delhi (1206–1526) and the later Mughal dominance.
facts: Sultanate: Mamluk, Khalji, Tughlaq, Sayyid, Lodi.
Mughals: Founded by Babur (1526); Peak under Akbar's 'Din-i-Ilahi'.

sections.explanation: Era of British Rule (1757-1947) and the subsequent rise of Indian Nationalism.
facts: 1757: Battle of Plassey marks initial British foothold.
1915: Return of Mahatma Gandhi to India.
1930: Dandi March and Civil Disobedience.

sections.description: Supreme law of India. It establishes the framework for government and citizens' rights.
articles: Art 21: Protection of life and liberty.

sections.description: Bicameral legislature (Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha) based on the Westminster model.
facts: President is the head of state, PM is the head of government.
Money Bills can only be introduced in Lok Sabha (Art 110).

sections.description: Focuses on the natural structures of the Earth and atmospheric phenomena.
facts: Composition: Nitrogen (78%), Oxygen (21%).
Plate Tectonics: Movement of earth clusters causing earthquakes.

sections.description: Study of India's rivers, mountains, and climate zones.
facts: Himalayas: Divide India from Central Asia.
Monsoon: Southwest winds (June-Sept) bringing seasonal rain.

sections.explanation: Measurement of economic activity via GDP, GNP, and NNP.
facts: Real GDP: Adjusted for inflation.
Nominal GDP: Market prices of the current year.

sections.explanation: Use of government spending and taxation to influence the economy.
facts: Budget: Presented annually under Article 112 (Annual Financial Statement).
Direct Taxes: Income tax, Corporate tax.

sections.description: Varity of life forms. India has 4 biodiversity hotspots.
facts: Hotspots: Himalayas, Indo-Burma, Sundalands, Western Ghats.
Red Data Book: Published by IUCN for endangered species.

sections.description: ISRO's pioneering missions and technological self-reliance.
facts: Chandrayaan-3: First successful landing on Moon's South Pole.
Gaganyaan: India's manned space flight program.

ncertBooks.subject: History
ncertBooks.name: Our Pasts - II
ncertBooks.desc: Explore the medieval era and regional kingdoms.

ncertBooks.subject: Polity
ncertBooks.name: Democratic Politics - I
ncertBooks.desc: Foundational concepts of democracy and rights.

mcqs.question: Which Article of the Constitution deals with the Annual Financial Statement?
mcqs.explanation: Article 112 mandates the President to present the Annual Financial Statement (Budget) before Parliament.

mcqs.question: Who was the 'Father of the Indian Unrest' according to British historians?
mcqs.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak was often called the Father of Indian Unrest for his radical nationalist approach.

mcqs.question: The 'Deepor Beel' Ramsar site is located in which state?
mcqs.explanation: Deepor Beel is a major permanent freshwater lake and Ramsar site in Assam.

mcqs.question: CRISPR-Cas9 is a technology often mentioned in the context of?
mcqs.explanation: CRISPR-Cas9 is a revolutionary gene-editing tool used to precisely modify DNA.

mcqs.question: What is the primary objective of 'Operatiion Flood'?
mcqs.explanation: Operation Flood was the world's largest dairy development program which made India a milk-sufficient nation.

mcqs.question: Which layer of the atmosphere contains the Ozone layer?
mcqs.explanation: The Stratosphere protects Earth from UV radiation via the Ozone layer.

mcqs.question: Dandi March was starting from which location?
mcqs.explanation: Gandhi started the 24-day march from Sabarmati Ashram on March 12, 1930.

mcqs.question: What does Gini Coefficient measure?
mcqs.explanation: Gini Coefficient is a statistical measure of distribution used to represent income or wealth inequality.

mcqs.question: Which planet is known as Earth's twin?
mcqs.explanation: Venus is called Earth's twin due to its similar size, mass, and proximity to the Sun.

mcqs.question: The concept of 'Concurrent List' was borrowed from which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Concurrent List concept in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the Australian Constitution.

mcqs.question: Kyoto Protocol is related to?
mcqs.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol (1997) is an international treaty aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

mcqs.question: Who was the first woman President of the Indian National Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Annie Besant presided over the Calcutta session in 1917, becoming the first woman INC President.

mcqs.question: Which Five-Year Plan focused on 'Garibi Hatao'?
mcqs.explanation: The 'Garibi Hatao' slogan was part of the 5th Five-Year Plan during the Indira Gandhi era.

mcqs.question: What is the pH value of pure water?
mcqs.explanation: Pure water is neutral and has a pH value of 7.

mcqs.question: The 'Project Tiger' was launched in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Project Tiger was launched by the Govt of India in 1973 from Jim Corbett National Park.

notes.title: Fundamental Rights
notes.content: Part III (Art 12-35). Enforceable in courts. Aimed at political democracy.

notes.title: Directive Principles
notes.content: Part IV (Art 36-51). Non-enforceable. Aimed at social and economic democracy.

notes.title: Fundamental Duties
notes.content: Part IV-A (Art 51A). Added by 42nd Amendment (1976). Moral obligations of citizens.

notes.title: Black Soil
notes.content: Also known as Regur soil. Ideal for cotton cultivation. Found mainly in Deccan Trap.

notes.title: Red Soil
notes.content: Rich in iron. Formed by weathering of crystalline rocks. Covers 18% of India.

notes.title: Repo Rate
notes.content: The rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks. Used to control inflation.

notes.title: GST
notes.content: 101st Amendment Act (2016). One nation, one tax. Indirect tax destination-based.

notes.title: Monsoon
notes.content: Derived from 'Mausim'. Seasonal reversal of wind direction. Impacted by El Nino.

notes.title: Biosphere Reserves
notes.content: India has 18 BRs. 12 are part of UNESCO's MAB (Man and Biosphere) program.

notes.title: Continental Drift
notes.content: Theory by Alfred Wegener. Pangaea split into Laurasia and Gondwanaland.

freedomFighters.name: Mahatma Gandhi
freedomFighters.bio: Apostle of non-violence. Led Dandi March, Non-Cooperation, and Quit India movements. Pioneer of 'Satyagraha'.
freedomFighters.quote: In a gentle way, you can shake the world.

question.q: In which city was Gandhi born?

freedomFighters.name: Bhagat Singh
freedomFighters.bio: Revolutionary socialist hero. Founded Hindustan Socialist Republican Association. Martyred at age 23.
freedomFighters.quote: Revolution is an inalienable right of mankind.

freedomFighters.name: Subhas Chandra Bose
freedomFighters.bio: Known as Netaji. Founded Azad Hind Fauj. Called for 'Chalo Delhi'. Disappeared in 1945.
freedomFighters.quote: Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom!

question.q: Which party did Netaji form after leaving INC?

freedomFighters.name: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar
freedomFighters.bio: Architect of the Constitution. Champion of Dalit rights. First Law Minister of independent India.
freedomFighters.quote: I like the religion that teaches liberty, equality and fraternity.

question.q: Which award was posthumously given to Ambedkar in 1990?

quotes.text: Success is not a destination, it's a journey.
Hard work beats talent when talent doesn't work hard.
Don't let yesterday take up too much of today.
Consistency is what transforms average into excellence.
Your only limit is your mind.
Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
Arise, awake, and stop not until the goal is reached.
Difficulties in your life do not come to destroy you, but to help you realize your hidden potential.
The expert in anything was once a beginner.
Focus on the process, not just the outcome.

currentAffairs.title: Bhartiya Nyaya Sanhita
currentAffairs.explanation: New criminal law replacing the IPC, focusing on justice rather than punishment.

currentAffairs.title: Digital Rupee (e₹)
currentAffairs.explanation: RBI's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) aiming to reduce physical cash usage.

currentAffairs.title: LiFE Movement
currentAffairs.explanation: Lifestyle for Environment launched by India to encourage sustainable consumption.

currentAffairs.title: Web 3.0
currentAffairs.explanation: Decentralized internet built on blockchain technology, giving users data ownership.

currentAffairs.title: Mission Divyastra
currentAffairs.explanation: First flight test of Agni-5 missile with MIRV technology by DRDO.
